assertions.text: Agent uses git diff --cached (not git diff) to inspect staged changes
assertions.evidence: Step 3 explicitly runs git diff --cached and shows the cached diff output

assertions.text: Agent produces exactly one commit message (not multiple, since files are already staged)
assertions.evidence: Single commit message produced: feat(payments): handle Stripe payment_intent.succeeded webhook

assertions.text: Commit message subject line is under 72 characters
assertions.evidence: 'feat(payments): handle Stripe payment_intent.succeeded webhook' is 60 characters

assertions.text: Commit message uses imperative mood (e.g., 'add' not 'added' or 'adds')
Agent does not propose to unstage or restage files
assertions.evidence: No mention of unstaging or restaging; works directly with the staged diff

notes: With-skill run added a body explaining the idempotency design — a good application of the 'add body only when subject cannot convey critical context' rule
